perturbation.original_text: 2. Definition. “Confidential Information” means any information, technical data or know-how, including, but not limited to, that which relates to research, products, services, customers, markets, software, developments, inventions, processes, designs, drawings, engineering, marketing or finances, disclosed orally or in written or electronic form, and which is marked or identified by the disclosing party as “proprietary” or “confidential”.
perturbation.changed_text: 2. Definition. “Confidential Information” means any information, technical data or know-how, including, but not limited to, that which relates to research, products, services, customers, markets, software, developments, inventions, processes, designs, drawings, engineering, marketing or finances, disclosed orally or in written or electronic form, regardless of whether it is marked or identified by the disclosing party as “proprietary” or “confidential”.
perturbation.explanation: This changes the definition of 'Confidential Information' to include information that is *not* marked as proprietary or confidential. This contradicts the implicit requirement established in the original definition, which implies that information must be explicitly marked as confidential to be considered such under the agreement. The in-text contradiction lies in how section 3 refers to Confidential Information.
perturbation.contradicted_text: 3. Non-disclosure of Confidential Information. The Company and Third Party each agree not to use the Confidential Information disclosed to it by the other party for its own use or for any purpose except to carry out discussions concerning and the undertaking of any business relationship between the two. The recipient of Confidential Information will not disclose such Confidential Information to anyone, including to their employees; however, the recipient of Confidential Information may disclose such information to certain employees who are required to have such information to carry out the contemplated business. Each has had or will have employees to whom Confidential Information of the other is disclosed sign a Non-disclosure Agreement in content substantially similar to this Agreement and will notify the other in writing of the names of the persons who have had access to Confidential Information of the other party. Each agrees that it will take all reasonable steps to protect the secrecy of and avoid disclosure or use of Confidential Information of the other in order to prevent it from falling into the public domain or the possession of unauthorised persons. Each agrees to notify the other in writing of any misuse or misappropriation of Confidential Information of the other that may come to its attention.
perturbation.justification: The original text defines 'Confidential Information' as information marked as 'proprietary' or 'confidential'. The changed text expands this definition to include information regardless of whether it's marked as such. Section 3, the 'Contradicted Text,' discusses the non-disclosure of 'Confidential Information' disclosed by either party. Since the changed text broadens the definition of 'Confidential Information,' it implies that even unmarked information must be protected under Section 3, creating an in-text contradiction. This contradiction arises because Section 3's obligations now apply to a broader set of information, potentially encompassing data not originally intended to be protected, thus making enforcement and interpretation unclear, as well as going against the implicitly intention of the original agreement.

perturbation.original_text: 3. Non-disclosure of Confidential Information. The Company and Third Party each agree not to use the Confidential Information disclosed to it by the other party for its own use or for any purpose except to carry out discussions concerning and the undertaking of any business relationship between the two. The recipient of Confidential Information will not disclose such Confidential Information to anyone, including to their employees; however, the recipient of Confidential Information may disclose such information to certain employees who are required to have such information to carry out the contemplated business.
perturbation.changed_text: 3. Non-disclosure of Confidential Information. The Company and Third Party each agree not to use the Confidential Information disclosed to it by the other party for its own use or for any purpose except to carry out discussions concerning and the undertaking of any business relationship between the two. The recipient of Confidential Information is encouraged, but not required, to avoid disclosing such Confidential Information to anyone, including to their employees; however, the recipient of Confidential Information may disclose such information to any employees or consultants without any obligations to keep it private or confidential.
perturbation.explanation: This modifies the obligation regarding non-disclosure to employees. Originally, disclosure to employees was allowed only if they needed the information and were under a similar NDA. The altered text removes any obligation to maintain confidentiality when sharing with employees and consultants. This contradicts the overall purpose of the NDA, creating an in-text contradiction because the agreement is about protecting confidential information, yet this section allows for unfettered disclosure to internal parties without needing an agreement or any safeguard.
perturbation.contradicted_text: 3. Non-disclosure of Confidential Information. The Company and Third Party each agree not to use the Confidential Information disclosed to it by the other party for its own use or for any purpose except to carry out discussions concerning and the undertaking of any business relationship between the two. The recipient of Confidential Information is encouraged, but not required, to avoid disclosing such Confidential Information to anyone, including to their employees; however, the recipient of Confidential Information may disclose such information to any employees or consultants without any obligations to keep it private or confidential. Each has had or will have employees to whom Confidential Information of the other is disclosed sign a Non-disclosure Agreement in content substantially similar to this Agreement and will notify the other in writing of the names of the persons who have had access to Confidential Information of the other party. Each agrees that it will take all reasonable steps to protect the secrecy of and avoid disclosure or use of Confidential Information of the other in order to prevent it from falling into the public domain or the possession of unauthorised persons. Each agrees to notify the other in writing of any misuse or misappropriation of Confidential Information of the other that may come to its attention.
perturbation.justification: The changed text removes the obligation to maintain confidentiality when sharing information with employees and consultants, stating that disclosure can occur 'without any obligations to keep it private or confidential.' This directly contradicts the contradicted text, which states that employees to whom Confidential Information is disclosed will sign a Non-disclosure Agreement and that the company will take 'all reasonable steps to protect the secrecy' of the information. The altered text creates a situation where confidential information can be freely shared without any safeguards, making the NDA essentially pointless and causing an in-text contradiction regarding the protection of confidential information.

perturbation.original_text: 5. Return of Materials. Any materials or documents that have been furnished by one party to the other will be promptly returned, accompanied by all copies of such documentation, after the business possibility has been rejected or concluded.
perturbation.changed_text: 5. Return of Materials. Any materials or documents that have been furnished by one party to the other may be retained indefinitely, and the recipient has no obligation to return any copies, regardless of whether the business possibility has been rejected or concluded.
perturbation.explanation: This modifies the obligation regarding the return of materials. Originally, all materials had to be returned. The revised text states that the recipient can keep the materials indefinitely. This introduces an in-text contradiction with Section 4 'Ownership'. If Ownership remains exclusive to the Disclosing Party, then the Recipient party should not have indefinite use of it.
perturbation.contradicted_text: 4. Ownership. All Confidential Information shall remain the exclusive property of Disclosing Party, and Recipient shall have no right to use Confidential Information except as provided herein. No patent, copyright, trademark or other proprietary right or licence is conveyed by this Agreement with respect to Confidential Information.
perturbation.justification: The changed text states that materials furnished by one party to the other may be retained indefinitely, regardless of the business possibility being rejected or concluded. This directly contradicts Section 4, which states that all Confidential Information shall remain the exclusive property of the Disclosing Party, and the Recipient shall have no right to use Confidential Information except as provided in the agreement. The change allows the recipient to indefinitely retain materials owned by the disclosing party, conflicting with the ownership clause.

perturbation.original_text: 6. Intellectual Property Rights. Nothing in this Agreement is intended to grant any rights under any patent or copyright of either party, nor shall this Agreement grant either party any rights in or to the other partyʼs Confidential Information, except the limited right to review such Confidential Information solely for the purposes of determining whether to enter into the proposed business relationship between the parties and in carrying out such relationship.
perturbation.changed_text: 6. Intellectual Property Rights. This agreement grants the receiving party a non-exclusive, royalty-free license to use the disclosing party’s Confidential Information for any purpose, including commercial exploitation, after the termination of this agreement.
perturbation.explanation: This change directly contradicts the original statement that the agreement does *not* grant any rights under any patent or copyright or to the other party’s confidential information. Granting a license, especially post-termination, directly conflicts with the stated purpose of the NDA to protect confidential information and intellectual property. It contradicts section 4, which specifies the the rights of the property.
perturbation.contradicted_text: 4. Ownership. All Confidential Information shall remain the exclusive property of Disclosing Party, and Recipient shall have no right to use Confidential Information except as provided herein. No patent, copyright, trademark or other proprietary right or licence is conveyed by this Agreement with respect to Confidential Information.
perturbation.justification: The changed text in section 6 grants the receiving party a license to use the disclosing party's Confidential Information after termination, directly contradicting both the original text of section 6, which states that the agreement doesn't grant any rights to confidential information, and section 4, which states that all confidential information remains the exclusive property of the disclosing party and that no license is conveyed by the agreement. This creates a clear conflict and uncertainty about the rights to confidential information, fitting the definition of an in-text contradiction.

perturbation.original_text: 8. Term. This Agreement may be terminated at all times with a 30 daysʼ prior written notice provided, however, that the confidentiality obligations herein shall terminate ____________ years following the date of termination of this Agreement.
perturbation.changed_text: 8. Term. This Agreement may be terminated at all times with a 30 daysʼ prior written notice; however, all confidentiality obligations herein immediately cease upon the date of termination of this Agreement.
perturbation.explanation: This modifies when confidentiality obligations terminate. Originally, they extended for a certain number of years after termination. Now, they cease immediately upon termination. The contradiction is that if the Receiving party does have access to confidential information as per section 3, after termination and all obligations are immediately ceased upon the date, there is no obligation for them to keep the information safe or secure, leading to breaches.
perturbation.contradicted_text: 3. Non-disclosure of Confidential Information. The Company and Third Party each agree not to use the Confidential Information disclosed to it by the other party for its own use or for any purpose except to carry out discussions concerning and the undertaking of any business relationship between the two. The recipient of Confidential Information will not disclose such Confidential Information to anyone, including to their employees; however, the recipient of Confidential Information may disclose such information to certain employees who are required to have such information to carry out the contemplated business. Each has had or will have employees to whom Confidential Information of the other is disclosed sign a Non-disclosure Agreement in content substantially similar to this Agreement and will notify the other in writing of the names of the persons who have had access to Confidential Information of the other party. Each agrees that it will take all reasonable steps to protect the secrecy of and avoid disclosure or use of Confidential Information of the other in order to prevent it from falling into the public domain or the possession of unauthorised persons. Each agrees to notify the other in writing of any misuse or misappropriation of Confidential Information of the other that may come to its attention.
perturbation.justification: The original text stated that confidentiality obligations terminate a certain number of years following the date of termination. The changed text states that all confidentiality obligations immediately cease upon the date of termination of the Agreement. This directly contradicts section 3, which outlines the ongoing obligations to protect confidential information and prevent its misuse. If the confidentiality obligations cease immediately upon termination, there is no longer a contractual basis for the receiving party to maintain the secrecy of the confidential information as required by section 3. Therefore, this constitutes a strong in-text contradiction.

perturbation.original_text: 10. Remedies. Each party agrees that its obligations hereunder are necessary and reasonable in order to protect the other party and the other partyʼs business, and expressly agrees that monetary damages would be inadequate to compensate the other party for any breach of any covenant or agreement set forth herein. Accordingly, each party agrees and acknowledges that any such violation or threatened violation will cause irreparable injury to the other party and that, in addition to any other remedies that may be available, in law, at equity or otherwise, the other party shall be entitled to obtain injunctive relief against the threatened breach of the Agreement or the continuation of any such breach, without the necessity of proving actual damages.
perturbation.changed_text: 10. Remedies. Each party agrees that its obligations hereunder are suggestions to protect the other party and the other party’s business, and agrees that monetary damages are the only compensation that the other party is entitled to for any breach of any covenant or agreement set forth herein. Accordingly, each party agrees and acknowledges that any such violation or threatened violation is not a serious issue to the other party and that, the other party shall be entitled to obtain monetary relief against the threatened breach of the Agreement or the continuation of any such breach and will have to prove actual damages.
perturbation.explanation: This modifies the remedies available in case of a breach. Originally, injunctive relief was available without proving actual damages, acknowledging the irreparable harm. Now, only monetary damages are available, and actual damages must be proven. This directly contradicts the assertion of 'irreparable injury', making the enforcement of the agreement uncertain. It also introduces an in-text contradiction with section 3: 'Non-disclosure of Confidential Information' where it details the measures of how to protect the confidentiality of information, and if there is a breach that leads to a violation and the violation is not a 'serious issue to the other party', then the importance of that section is undermined.
perturbation.contradicted_text: 3. Non-disclosure of Confidential Information. The Company and Third Party each agree not to use the Confidential Information disclosed to it by the other party for its own use or for any purpose except to carry out discussions concerning and the undertaking of any business relationship between the two. The recipient of Confidential Information will not disclose such Confidential Information to anyone, including to their employees; however, the recipient of Confidential Information may disclose such information to certain employees who are required to have such information to carry out the contemplated business. Each has had or will have employees to whom Confidential Information of the other is disclosed sign a Non-disclosure Agreement in content substantially similar to this Agreement and will notify the other in writing of the names of the persons who have had access to Confidential Information of the other party. Each agrees that it will take all reasonable steps to protect the secrecy of and avoid disclosure or use of Confidential Information of the other in order to prevent it from falling into the public domain or the possession of unauthorised persons. Each agrees to notify the other in writing of any misuse or misappropriation of Confidential Information of the other that may come to its attention.
perturbation.justification: The changed text significantly alters the remedies available for a breach of contract. Originally, injunctive relief was available without proving actual damages, which is consistent with the confidential information. However, the changed text limits the remedies to monetary damages only and requires proof of actual damages. This contradicts the confidential information that could result in 'serious' implications as outlined in section 3. By undermining the severity of a violation, the changed text introduces an in-text contradiction with Section 3, which emphasizes the importance of protecting confidential information and the measures taken to prevent its misuse.
